definition: The sub-investment-grade maturity wall ($900B-$1.5T, 2025-2028) interacts asymmetrically with the correlation regime: orderly refinancing without recession, potentially systemic with recession coincidence. Refinancing at 200-400bp higher generates ~$50-75B incremental annual interest expense.
evidence: Conditional base rate: 0/4 orderly without recession, 2/2 systemic with recession
~$2.50-3.75 S&P 500 EPS headwind concentrated in S&P 493
Credit availability channel: CLO formation constraints + bank lending limits → weakest borrowers face 10-12% all-in from private credit
Relevant figure is $900B-$1.5T sub-IG, not $2.5T total corporate debt
open_questions: What recession probability makes the maturity wall systemically relevant?
Can extend-and-pretend via private credit delay the wall beyond 2028?
How does the EPS headwind distribute across sectors?
